name: Chief of Staff Briefing
description: A static AgentSite chief-of-staff briefing for autonomous maintenance decisions.
brief: Create a static GitHub Pages site that demonstrates a chief-of-staff briefing generated from public-safe AgentSite data: requests, roadmap, runs, atlas, and search index. It should show top three next actions, risk flags, stale or blocked work, recent verified wins, and the recommended recipe to apply next without live monitoring, private tickets, raw chats, auth, analytics, or a database.
heroHeadline: A static chief-of-staff brief for the next agent run
heroLede: The page turns requests, roadmap items, run history, proof sites, and search metadata into a compact control brief for autonomous maintenance decisions.
audience: Technical reviewers evaluating static maintenance loops
visualDirection: dark cockpit executive briefing with risk flags and action panels

sections.title: The chief-of-staff view starts with decisions
sections.body: The generated briefing surfaces the few actions and risks an orchestrator should review before delegating work.

sections.title: Static signals replace vague status chatter
sections.body: Requests, roadmap, runs, atlas, and search metadata are transformed into a curated public-safe operating summary.

sections.title: No live monitoring or private intake
sections.body: The panel is generated from committed static data, not private tickets, raw chats, analytics, telemetry, auth, or a database.

proofArtifacts.label: Briefing builder
proofArtifacts.body: scripts/build-briefing.mjs generates src/data/briefing.json from public-safe static sources.

proofArtifacts.label: Briefing QA
proofArtifacts.body: scripts/check-briefing.mjs validates top actions, risk flags, wins, recommendation, and source counts.

proofArtifacts.label: Briefing UI
proofArtifacts.body: src/components/ChiefOfStaffBriefing.astro renders the executive panel and source evidence links.

allowedClaims: Repo-local QA scripts validate briefing records
approvalRequired: Adding analytics, cookies, tracking pixels, or third-party forms
Adding payment links or enabling payment mode
Indexing private tickets, raw chats, credentials, secrets, or unpublished user data
Adding live monitoring, server runtime, authentication, telemetry, or a database
